vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1358

plan.title: fix(skills): swarm Phase 6 must commit+push vBRIEF lifecycle moves to master after merge cascade

narratives.Description: After a swarm merge cascade, the vBRIEF lifecycle moves (active/ to completed/) are not committed and pushed as part of Phase 6, so they sit uncommitted until an operator hand-runs scope:complete and a chore(vbrief) commit. This story makes the Phase 6 close-out deterministic by mandating scope:complete plus a single chore(vbrief) commit and push to the base branch, preventing the release ceremony's lifecycle-sync gate from flagging false drift.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

After a swarm run, the vBRIEF lifecycle closeout (`task scope:complete` + commit to master) was not performed as part of Phase 6. The vBRIEFs remained in `vbrief/active/` on master after both PRs merged, requiring a manual `chore(vbrief)` commit by the operator after the fact.

## What happened

- Two vBRIEFs (#1353, #1331) were activated via `task scope:activate` before swarm dispatch (correct).
- Agents pushed their PRs and the monitor merged them via Phase 6.
- After both PRs merged, the monitor did NOT run `task scope:complete` on the vBRIEFs or push the lifecycle moves to master.
- The vBRIEFs sat in `vbrief/active/` on master until the operator noticed and ran `task scope:complete` manually, requiring a separate `chore(vbrief)` commit and push.

## Expected behavior

Phase 6 Step 2 (Close Issues and Update Origins) of `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` already says to move vBRIEFs via `task scope:complete`. But the step does not explicitly require the monitor to **commit and push** the resulting lifecycle moves to master as a Phase 6 post-merge action. The skill says "move the vBRIEF from active/ to completed/" but the commit discipline is not enforced.

## Proposed fix

Add a mandatory step to swarm Phase 6 Step 2 (or a new Phase 6 Step 2b):

> ! After all PRs are merged, for each completed scope vBRIEF:
> 1. Run `task scope:complete <file>` to move it from `vbrief/active/` to `vbrief/completed/`
> 2. Stage and commit all lifecycle moves in a single `chore(vbrief): complete <slugs> post-merge` commit directly to the base branch
> 3. Push to origin

This commit is the authoritative post-swarm lifecycle record and prevents the release ceremony's `check_vbrief_lifecycle_sync` gate from flagging drift.

## Why it matters

Without this, the release ceremony's Step 3 (`check_vbrief_lifecycle_sync`) will catch the drift and block the release until the operator manually closes the loop. That's a friction point that should be eliminated by making the swarm Phase 6 close-out deterministic.

## Related

- `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` Phase 5 "Complete vBRIEFs" + Phase 6 Step 2
- `scripts/reconcile_issues.py` (`--apply-lifecycle-fixes`)
- Release pre-flight Step 3 (`check_vbrief_lifecycle_sync`)
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Add a mandatory step to skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 (Step 2 / a new Step 2b) requiring that, after all PRs merge, the monitor for each completed scope vBRIEF: (1) runs task scope:complete <file> to move active/ -> completed/, (2) stages and commits all lifecycle moves in a single 'chore(vbrief): complete <slugs> post-merge' commit to the base branch, and (3) pushes to origin.
- Frame it as the authoritative post-swarm lifecycle record that prevents the release ceremony's check_vbrief_lifecycle_sync gate from flagging drift; cross-reference scripts/reconcile_issues.py --apply-lifecycle-fixes and the release skill Phase 1 sync gate.
- Add a content test in tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py asserting Phase 6 contains the commit+push lifecycle directive (scope:complete + chore(vbrief) commit + push). Re-render packs (task packs:render).
- Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry in #1242 style referencing #1358.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want Phase 6 to deterministically commit and push the vBRIEF lifecycle moves to the base branch after the merge cascade, so that the next release ceremony's lifecycle-sync gate does not flag drift.
narratives.Approach: 2026-06-17 file-overlap audit: touches skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md + tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py only. The rendered packs/skills-pack-0.1.json is a generated artifact re-rendered via task packs:render and reconciled at merge (not listed in file_scope). Disjoint primary scope from the review-cycle carrier (different skill) and the code carriers. Dogfooding motivation: this exact manual chore(vbrief) closeout was performed by hand after the 2026-06-16 swarm. Parallel-safe; no depends_on.

items.title: Phase 6 mandates scope:complete + chore(vbrief) commit + push after the cascade.

narrative.Acceptance: Given all cohort PRs merged, when the operator follows Phase 6, then the skill requires task scope:complete per vBRIEF, a single chore(vbrief) commit of the lifecycle moves to the base branch, and a push (asserted in tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py).

items.title: Cross-reference to the release lifecycle-sync gate.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the skill text, when reviewed, then it explains this closeout prevents the release ceremony's check_vbrief_lifecycle_sync gate from flagging drift.

items.title: Content test asserts the commit+push directive is present.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the test suite, when pytest tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py runs, then it asserts the Phase 6 commit+push lifecycle directive and fails before the change.
Given the diff, when reviewed, then packs are in sync and CHANGELOG.md has an [Unreleased] entry referencing #1358.

references.title: Issue #1358: fix(skills): swarm Phase 6 must commit+push vBRIEF lifecycle moves to master after merge cascade

expected_outputs: Phase 6 requires scope:complete + chore(vbrief) commit + push
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Bug ingested from GitHub issue #1358; no spec-section trace applies. File scope disjoint from other cohort carriers.
